title: قرار رقم 31: بشأن اعتماد الإصدار رقم (2,0) لسياسة نقل الأرقام

provisions.title: القرار رقم (22)لسنة 2009
provisions.content: -الإلغاءاتيُلغىالقرار رقم (22)لسنة 2009 المشارإليه ،كما يُلغى أينص في أي قرارآخر إلى المدىالذي يتعارضفيه وأحكامهذا القرار.

provisions.title: يُعملبهذا القرارمن تاريخصدوره،ويُنشر فيالجريدةالرسمية.
provisions.content: -السريانوالنشريُعملبهذا القرارمن تاريخصدوره،ويُنشر فيالجريدةالرسمية.صدربتاريخ:24/أكتوبر/2022مطلالحميدبالهولرئيسمجلسالإدارةنشرهذا القرار فيعدد الجريدةالرسمية رقم738.ص79.RegulatoryPolicyNumberPortabilityVersion2.0IssueDate: 24 Oct. 2022RevisionControl:Version:IssueDate:Reasonfor Revision:2.024Oct. 2022Amendmentsbased on implementation of Number Portability on several types of numberresources (mainly articles 2.1 and 7.2), along with other correctiveedits.1.105thAugust 2009Amendmentsin association with other regulatory instruments and based on the progress inthe Industry Group for Number Portability (IGNP)1.011thApril 20061- Definition1.1 Allwords, terms and phrases used in this Regulatory Policy shall have the meaningsascribed to them, if any, in the Federal Law by Decree no 3 of 2003 as amendedregarding the Organization of the Telecommunications Sector or the TDRA’sPolicy for National Numbering Plan (as amended) or any replacement for either ofthem, unless otherwise provided for in this Regulatory Policy.1.2 Thefollowing words and phrases shall have the meaning ascribed to them below:AllCall Query (ACQ) The functionality permitting Licensees to query adatabase copy of the Centralized Data Base in order to identify PortedNumbers.CentralizedData Base (CDB) A centrally held data base of Ported Numbers which is thereference for Licensees for ACQ.DonorLicensee The Licensee that provides a Telecommunications Service to aSubscriber using a particular Number, prior to Porting the Number.FixedNumber A Number associated with a Subscriber for the use of aTelecommunication Service, where that Number is, in accordance with the NationalNumbering Plan, of the Format (OY XXXXXXX, where Y is 2, 3, 4, 6, 7 or 9).Licensee(s)An entity licensed at the date of the coming into force of this Policy by TDRApursuant to the provisions of the Federal Law by Decree No. 3 of 2003 as amendedand its Executive Order.MobileNumber A Number associated with a Subscriber for the use of aTelecommunications Service, where that Number is in accordance with the NationalNumbering Plan, of the format (05X XXXXXXX).MobileNumber Portability Number Portability involving a Mobile Number.NumberPortability Regulatory Framework issued by the TDRA in connection withthe implementation ofRegulationsNumber Portability.NumberPorting System A centralized system used by Licensees for the Porting ofNumbers that comprises a CDB and an Order Handling Platform.NumberA number associated with a Subscriber for the use of Telecommunication Service,which number is allocated to a party in accordance with the National NumberingPlan.OrderHandling Platform A centralized platform that supports Number Portabilityprocesses among Licensees for res¬ponding to and initiating Port requestsfrom Subscribers, including without limitation the ex¬change of messagesamong participating Licensees, validation of these messages, and thenotification of executed Ports to all Licensees.NewLicensees Entities obtaining a Licence after the coming into force ofthis Policy.NumberPortability The capability or service whereby a Subscriber who is beingprovided with a Telecommunications Service in association or connection with aparticular Number, can terminate the contract with the Licensee providing thatTelecommunications Service, but continue to use that Number in association orconnection with the provision of a Telecommunications Service by anotherLicensee.OutageLimits Shall mean the acceptable periods of time during whichTelecommunication Services may be unavailable (in full or in part) toSubscribers during the Porting process, which periods shall be determined by theTDRA.Number– Reserve – Period A period of time during which a Numbershall not be re-used after the provision of a Telecommunications Serviceutilising that Number has ceased, as specified in the Regulatory Framework ineffect at the time and in particular the National Numbering Plan Policy.PortWith respect to a specific Number, the retention by the relevant Subscriber ofthe use of that Number while following a change in the identity of the Licenseeproviding the relevant Telecommunications Service, and the terms“ Porting ”,“ Ported ” and“ Porting Process ” shall allhave corresponding meanings.PrepaidServices A Telecommunications Service where the relevant Licensee allowsthe relevant Subscriber to pay for the Telecommunications Service in advance ofthe actual use of the Telecommunications Service.PostpaidServices A Telecommunications Service where the relevant Licensee allowsthe relevant Subscriber to pay for a significant portion of services theTelecommunications Service subsequent to the actual use of theTelecommunications Service.RangeHolder The Licensee that has been allocated the Number range/blockcontaining the relevant Ported Number.RecipientLed Porting A process for Number Portability under which the Subscriberwishing to Port a Number must contact the Recipient Licensee which will thenarrange the process with the Donor Licensee. From the Subscriber’s pointof view this will be a seamless process requiring that Subscriber only makecontact with the Recipient Licensee.RecipientLicensee The Licensee that provides a Telecommunications Service to aSubscriber in association with a particular Number following the Porting of thatNumber.RegulatoryFramework The regulatory policies, regulations, instructions, orders,resolutions, procedures, guidelines and other such determinations as may be madeor amended by the TDRA.Subscriber(s)An entity that orders and is responsible for paying for a TelecommunicationService from a Licensee where the Subscriber does not:-resell that Telecommunication Service to another; and-include that Telecommunication Service in or as part of anotherTelecommunicationService (whether as an essential input of commercial; technical or other nature)which it then sells to another.TelecommunicationService As defined in the Federal Law by Decree No. 3 of 2003 as amendedor any subsequent revisions or new Telecommunications Laws or related laws asmay be issued.TDRAThe Telecommunication and Digital Government Regulatory Authority.2- Scopeof Policy2.1 AllSubscribers are entitled to Port Number(s) allocated to them regardless of whichLicensee is providing those Subscribers with the relevant TelecommunicationServices. Number Portability is available in the following instances:2.1.1 inthe case of geographic Numbers (or Fixed Numbers) at a specific location;and2.1.2 inthe case of Mobile Numbers at any location.2.1.3 inthe case of other Numbers if TDRA decides to include such Numbers in NumberPortability.Forthe avoidance of doubt, unless instructed otherwise by TDRA in writing, thiswill mean that Licensees must make available Number Portability to Subscribersof Telecommunications Services whether the relevant Numbers are geographic,non-geographic or Mobile Numbers, and regardless of whether or not the relevantTelecommunications Services are Prepaid Services or Postpaid Services.2.2 Licenseesshall make Number Portability available under reasonable prices, terms andconditions to any of their Subscribers who so request, provided that theprovisions of this Regulatory Policy, the Number Portability Regulations and anyother relevant regulatory requirements are met.2.3 Forthe avoidance of doubt, unless the TDRA decides otherwise, this RegulatoryPolicy will not apply to New Licensees.3- Implementation3.1 Licenseesshall participate in an industry forum with the objective of developing andmaintaining detailed technical and administrative specifications for NumberPortability. This industry forum shall commence and re- commence at datesnotified by the TDRA to the Licensees.3.2 Licenseesshall implement Number Portability at a date to be determined by the TDRA andnotified to the Licensees.4- ChargesAnydirect or indirect charges levied by the Licensees in relation to NumberPortability shall not be such as act as a disincentive to the Porting of Numbersby Subscribers. Where required, the TDRA shall specify charges, terms andconditions applicable relating to the provision of Number Portability orassociated Telecommunication Services.5- CostsCostsand expenses for the implementation and carrying out of Number Portabilityincurred by the Licensees and other entities shall be recovered by, andapportioned amongst those Licensees and other entities, in accordance with theRegulatory Framework in effect at the time.6- Provisioning6.1 Unlessthe TDRA decides otherwise the appropriate process for Number Portability shallbe Recipient Led Porting.6.2 Accordingly,it is the responsibility of the Recipient Licensee to take all reasonable stepsto ensure that the person requesting the Porting of a Number is either therelevant Subscriber, or in fact authorized on behalf of the relevant Subscriberto do so.6.3 TheLicensees shall comply with any Outage Limits which the TDRA may set from timeto time.6.4 Inany event, unless approved otherwise by TDRA, the Licensees shall provideTelecommunication Services to all Subscribers (regardless of whether or not suchTelecommunications Services are provided to a Ported or a non-Ported Number) ona non-discriminatory basis, and at the same published tariffs and on the sameterms and conditions as are applied to the provision of the same type ofTelecommunication Services to all other Subscribers.7- Feesfor the Ported Numbers7.1 Theoriginal Range Holder, which has been assigned Numbers by the TDRA, shall remainthe Range Holder regardless of the Subscriber Porting unless the entirerange/block has been Ported to the other Licensee.7.2 Theannual fee, which is payable in advance on the first day of January of each yearand due to the TDRA by a Licensee for a Number allocated to that Licensee by theTDRA pursuant to the National Numbering Plan, and the Fees for the Allocationand Use of Number Resources Policies, shall, where that Number has been Ported,be payable by the Licensee from whom the Subscriber is receiving the relevantTelecommunication service at the relevant time calculated and adjusted as deemedappropriate by TDRA (Such adjustment may be applied at the beginning of the yearonly, and ported in numbers could be considered as if those numbers wereselected by the new serving licensee for fee adjustment purposes).7.3 Theannual fee for a Number during its Number-Reserve-Period will be paid by theLicensee from whom the Subscriber was receiving the relevant telecommunicationservice before the Number was put into Number- Reserve-Period.7.4 PortedNumbers shall remain in Number-Reserve-Period before being returned to the RangeHolder for re-allocation in accordance to the Regulatory Framework in effect atthe time.8- Fraudand Credit WorthinessTheLicensees shall work cooperatively with regard to TDRA approved matters of fraudand credit worthiness,9- Useof InformationTheLicensees shall not use any information about subscribers, gathered or gained asa result of the implementation or provision of Number Portability, for anyreasons other than the provisioning of Number Portability, and in particular,such information shall not be sold or given away or used for marketingpurposes.10- PeriodicReportingLicenseesand other entities supporting Number Portability shall provide any informationrequested, including periodic reports, to the TDRA in the format, manner and atthe time frames determined by the TDRA.11- EffectiveDateThisRegulatory Policy will be effective from the date of its issue.12- PublicationTheTDRA reserves the right to modify, amend or supplement this Regulatory Policy atany time.[1]انالقرار رقم 22تاريخ05/08/2009مبشأن إعتمادوإصدارالسياسةالتنظيميةبشأن خدمة نقلأرقامالهواتفالمتحركةالمذكورأعلاهوالملغىبموجب هذهالمادة ،منشور في عددالجريدةالرسمية رقم 497ص 421 ، فاقتضىالتنويه .
